pattern: Accumulate 1+2+...+k until the running total reaches or passes the absolute target; the leftover overshoot's parity tells you whether one or two extra moves are needed to flip a sign and land exactly.
hints: By symmetry only the distance from the origin matters, so work with the absolute value of the target.
Keep adding consecutive step sizes until the triangular sum is at least that distance; any overshoot is an even amount you can erase by flipping a chosen earlier step from + to -.
If the overshoot is even you are done at the current move count; if it is odd, advance one or two more moves until the gap becomes even, since flipping a step changes the total by an even number.

edge_inputs.description: small positive target needing three moves
target equal to a triangular number
negative target mirrors the positive case
target one is reachable in a single move
large target near the constraint extreme

stress.description: random targets across the full signed range including extremes
stress.note: avoids zero which is excluded by the constraints
